title: Imports from the same module should be merged
description: Imports from the same module should be mergedAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEes2015Why is this an issue?More InfoHaving the same module imported multiple times can affect code readability and maintainability. It makes hard to identify which modules are being used.import { B1 } from 'b'; import { B2 } from 'b'; // Noncompliant: there is already an import from module 'b'.Instead, one should consolidate the imports from the same module into a single statement. By consolidating all imports from the same module in a singleimportstatement, the code becomes more concise and easier to read, as there is only one import statement to keep track of.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

noncompliant:
import { B1 } from 'b';
import { B2 } from 'b';

import React from 'react';
import { useState } from 'react';

const fs = require('fs');
const { readFile } = require('fs');

import * as utils from 'lodash';
import { map } from 'lodash';
compliant: import * as utils from 'lodash'; // or just import { map } from 'lodash';
